channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: For years, scientists could only prove that DOGS existed for 10,000 years — until now
item.description: A new study genetically shows dogs existed almost 5,000 years earlier than scientists could prove.
item.content:encoded: A new study genetically shows dogs existed almost 5,000 years earlier than scientists could prove.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: NASA is just days away from historic Artemis II moon launch
item.description: On Wednesday, the crew of NASA's Artemis II could blast off on a mission around the moon and back. No astronaut has ventured out to the moon since the 1970s.
item.content:encoded:
On Wednesday, the crew of NASA's Artemis II could blast off on a mission around the moon and back. No astronaut has ventured out to the moon since the 1970s.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: Philanthropy is a 'significant form of power.' Here's how Jeffrey Epstein exploited that
item.description: A large share of science funding comes through philanthropy, with little legal or public scrutiny. This lack of oversight allowed Jeffrey Epstein to cultivate scientists and launder his reputation.
item.content:encoded:
A large share of science funding comes through philanthropy, with little legal or public scrutiny. This lack of oversight allowed Jeffrey Epstein to cultivate scientists and launder his reputation.

(Image credit: Hanna Barczyk for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Trump administration cuts turned rural towns into sitting ducks for disasters
item.description: The Trump administration has delayed billions of dollars for projects to protect Americans from floods, wildfires and hurricanes. Local leaders are increasingly anxious.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration has delayed billions of dollars for projects to protect Americans from floods, wildfires and hurricanes. Local leaders are increasingly anxious.

(Image credit: Mel Evans)
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Hersher

item.title: Scientists watch sperm whales work as a team to assist a birth
item.description: An unprecedented look at the birth of a sperm whale found that mother and calf were supported by other whales throughout the process.
item.content:encoded:
An unprecedented look at the birth of a sperm whale found that mother and calf were supported by other whales throughout the process.

(Image credit: Project CETI)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Trump's attacks on offshore wind could hurt infrastructure spending across the economy
item.description: President Trump has tried to kill offshore wind's future in the U.S. But industry analysts say the attacks could hurt business confidence across the U.S. economy.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has tried to kill offshore wind's future in the U.S. But industry analysts say the attacks could hurt business confidence across the U.S. economy.

(Image credit: DON EMMERT/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
item.description: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.
item.content:encoded:
Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.

(Image credit: Andy Wong/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The oldest known recording of a whale song reveals how oceans have changed
item.description: Researchers at the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution have uncovered the oldest known recording of whale song. And it reveals a noisier soundscape of today's oceans.
item.content:encoded: Researchers at the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution have uncovered the oldest known recording of whale song. And it reveals a noisier soundscape of today's oceans.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: This week's Short Wave news roundup
item.description: NPR's Short Wave team talks about a wildflower's ability to adapt to climate change, the grooming habits of birds, and the social lives of sharks.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Short Wave team talks about a wildflower's ability to adapt to climate change, the grooming habits of birds, and the social lives of sharks.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: New autism group meets to counter MAHA's 'ideological agenda'
item.description: Autism experts plan to convene in Washington Thursday to propose a research agenda at odds with the one endorsed by the Trump Administration.
item.content:encoded:
Autism experts plan to convene in Washington Thursday to propose a research agenda at odds with the one endorsed by the Trump Administration.

(Image credit: Tom Williams/CQ-Roll Call, Inc)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton
